2026-04-17T17:23:40.1123274Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T17:23:40.1123699Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T17:23:40.1124059Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T17:23:40.1124362Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T17:23:40.1124593Z [36;1m[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. 2026-04-17T17:24:57.7367538Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T17:24:42.659550141Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58303","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T17:24:57.7389001Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T17:24:42.889959872Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58303,\"title\":\"[Tables] GetEntityIfExistsAsync throws ArgumentNullException when response has no content body\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\n`TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync` throws `System.ArgumentNullException` with `Value cannot be null. (Parameter \\u0026#39;utf8Json\\u0026#39;)` when the service returns a non-404 response with a null `ContentStream`.\\n\\nBoth `partitionKey` and `rowKey` are non-null. Based on the [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.data.tables.tableclient.getentityifexistsasync), the expected behavior for this method is to either return a `NullableResponse` (with `HasValue = false` for not-found cases) or throw a `RequestFailedException` for unexpected errors. An `ArgumentNullException` from internal JSON parsing is unexpected and unhelpful for diagnosing the issue.\\n\\n### Stack trace\\n\\n```\\nSystem.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter \\u0026#39;utf8Json\\u0026#39;)\\n   at System.ArgumentNullException.Throw(String paramName)\\n   at System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(Stream utf8Json, JsonDocumentOptions options)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(Response response)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityInternalAsync[T](Boolean async, String partitionKey, String rowKey, Boolean noThrow, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync[T](String partitionKey, String rowKey, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n```\\n\\n## Analysis\\n\\nThe root cause is in [`GetEntityInternalAsync`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs#L791-L800). When the response is not a 404, the code unconditionally calls `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response)`:\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (response.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound)\\n{\\n    return new NoValueResponse(response);\\n}\\nelse\\n{\\n    var dictionary = SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response);  // line 797\\n    var result = dictionary.ToTableEntity();\\n    return Response.FromValue(result, response);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n[`ResponseToDictionary`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Extensions/SerializationHelpers.cs#L14) passes `response.ContentStream` directly to `JsonDocument.Parse` without a null check:\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal static Dictionary ResponseToDictionary(Response response)\\n{\\n    using var document = JsonDocument.Parse(response.ContentStream);  // throws if ContentStream is null\\n    ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nIf the HTTP response has a non-404 status code but a null `ContentStream` (which can happen due to service behavior, network intermediaries, or proxy/gateway interference), the `JsonDocument.Parse` call throws `ArgumentNullException`.\\n\\nThis same code path is shared by `GetEntityAsync`, though in that case the pipeline is more likely to throw `RequestFailedException` for error status codes before reaching the parse logic. For `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, the `noThrow` path uses `TablesRequestContext.CreateIfNotExists` which only classifies 404 as non-error — other unexpected status codes with empty bodies will reach the parse logic and throw the misleading `ArgumentNullException`.\\n\\n## Suggested Implementation\\n\\nAdd a null check for `response.ContentStream` in `GetEntityInternalAsync` before calling `ResponseToDictionary`. When the content stream is null on a non-404 response, throw a `RequestFailedException` with a descriptive message so callers can diagnose the issue:\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (response.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound)\\n{\\n    return new NoValueResponse(response);\\n}\\nelse\\n{\\n    if (response.ContentStream is null)\\n    {\\n        throw new RequestFailedException(\\n            response,\\n            $\\u0026#34;The service returned status code {response.Status} but no response body.\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    var dictionary = SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response);\\n    var result = dictionary.ToTableEntity();\\n    return Response.FromValue(result, response);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThis approach:\\n- Preserves the `RequestFailedException` contract documented on the method\\n- Provides a meaningful error message that helps users diagnose the root cause (e.g., identify proxy/gateway issues)\\n- Does not change behavior for the normal success path (non-null content stream)\\n- Could also be applied defensively in `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` as a secondary guard\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58303\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"bug\",\"Tables\"],\"comments\":1,\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T17:22:13Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T17:23:53Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-17T17:24:57.7407485Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T17:24:42.904184938Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":58303,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T17:24:57.7428824Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T17:24:43.06639701Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58303,\"title\":\"[Tables] GetEntityIfExistsAsync throws ArgumentNullException when response has no content body\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\n`TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync` throws `System.ArgumentNullException` with `Value cannot be null. (Parameter \\u0026#39;utf8Json\\u0026#39;)` when the service returns a non-404 response with a null `ContentStream`.\\n\\nBoth `partitionKey` and `rowKey` are non-null. Based on the [documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/azure.data.tables.tableclient.getentityifexistsasync), the expected behavior for this method is to either return a `NullableResponse` (with `HasValue = false` for not-found cases) or throw a `RequestFailedException` for unexpected errors. An `ArgumentNullException` from internal JSON parsing is unexpected and unhelpful for diagnosing the issue.\\n\\n### Stack trace\\n\\n```\\nSystem.ArgumentNullException: Value cannot be null. (Parameter \\u0026#39;utf8Json\\u0026#39;)\\n   at System.ArgumentNullException.Throw(String paramName)\\n   at System.Text.Json.JsonDocument.Parse(Stream utf8Json, JsonDocumentOptions options)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(Response response)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityInternalAsync[T](Boolean async, String partitionKey, String rowKey, Boolean noThrow, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n   at Azure.Data.Tables.TableClient.GetEntityIfExistsAsync[T](String partitionKey, String rowKey, IEnumerable`1 select, CancellationToken cancellationToken)\\n```\\n\\n## Analysis\\n\\nThe root cause is in [`GetEntityInternalAsync`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/TableClient.cs#L791-L800). When the response is not a 404, the code unconditionally calls `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response)`:\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (response.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound)\\n{\\n    return new NoValueResponse(response);\\n}\\nelse\\n{\\n    var dictionary = SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response);  // line 797\\n    var result = dictionary.ToTableEntity();\\n    return Response.FromValue(result, response);\\n}\\n```\\n\\n[`ResponseToDictionary`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/tables/Azure.Data.Tables/src/Extensions/SerializationHelpers.cs#L14) passes `response.ContentStream` directly to `JsonDocument.Parse` without a null check:\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal static Dictionary ResponseToDictionary(Response response)\\n{\\n    using var document = JsonDocument.Parse(response.ContentStream);  // throws if ContentStream is null\\n    ...\\n}\\n```\\n\\nIf the HTTP response has a non-404 status code but a null `ContentStream` (which can happen due to service behavior, network intermediaries, or proxy/gateway interference), the `JsonDocument.Parse` call throws `ArgumentNullException`.\\n\\nThis same code path is shared by `GetEntityAsync`, though in that case the pipeline is more likely to throw `RequestFailedException` for error status codes before reaching the parse logic. For `GetEntityIfExistsAsync`, the `noThrow` path uses `TablesRequestContext.CreateIfNotExists` which only classifies 404 as non-error — other unexpected status codes with empty bodies will reach the parse logic and throw the misleading `ArgumentNullException`.\\n\\n## Suggested Implementation\\n\\nAdd a null check for `response.ContentStream` in `GetEntityInternalAsync` before calling `ResponseToDictionary`. When the content stream is null on a non-404 response, throw a `RequestFailedException` with a descriptive message so callers can diagnose the issue:\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (response.Status == (int)HttpStatusCode.NotFound)\\n{\\n    return new NoValueResponse(response);\\n}\\nelse\\n{\\n    if (response.ContentStream is null)\\n    {\\n        throw new RequestFailedException(\\n            response,\\n            $\\u0026#34;The service returned status code {response.Status} but no response body.\\u0026#34;);\\n    }\\n    var dictionary = SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary(response);\\n    var result = dictionary.ToTableEntity();\\n    return Response.FromValue(result, response);\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThis approach:\\n- Preserves the `RequestFailedException` contract documented on the method\\n- Provides a meaningful error message that helps users diagnose the root cause (e.g., identify proxy/gateway issues)\\n- Does not change behavior for the normal success path (non-null content stream)\\n- Could also be applied defensively in `SerializationHelpers.ResponseToDictionary` as a secondary guard\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58303\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"jsquire\",\"id\":913445,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/jsquire\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/913445?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"bug\",\"Tables\"],\"comments\":1,\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-17T17:22:13Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-17T17:23:53Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
